titleRaw: New Main Road Axis, North Evia Spine (Psachna – Strofylia section)
displayTitle: New Main Road Axis, North Evia Spine (Psachna – Strofylia section)
description: Following the invitation of the Ministry of Infrastructure and Transport to the Region of Central Greece to submit a proposal for the preparation of the necessary studies for the first section of the northern road axis from Psachna to Strofyllia, the Region submitted the complete dossier on 25 November 2025, including the technical data sheets, supporting documentation and tender documents for the relevant studies. The submission deadline was 31 December 2025. The proposal is now pending evaluation by the competent authority, after which approval is expected, followed immediately by tendering.
comments: https://pste.gov.gr/deltio-typou-o-perifereiarchis-stereas-elladas-fanis-spanos-eiche-synantisi-ergasias-me-ton-ypourgo-ypodomon-metaforon-christo-dima-gia-tin-poreia-ylopoiisis-ergon-kai-meleton/

titleRaw: Restoration of infrastructures in forest recreation areas
displayTitle: Restoration of infrastructures in forest recreation areas
description: These areas were built by the Limni Forest Service over a period of about 15 years and now require urgent restoration after suffering damage in the devastating 2021 wildfires in northern Evia. Their rehabilitation will also improve their appearance and functionality, provide safe and attractive recreation spaces for visitors, support environmental education, and help boost tourism and the local economy without harming the natural environment.
locationArea:

titleRaw: Maintenance, signage and promotion of marked hiking routes in the Istiaia–Aidipsos municipality area
displayTitle: Maintenance, signage and promotion of marked hiking routes in the Istiaia–Aidipsos municipality area
description: The project aims to attract visitors from Greece and abroad to the Municipality of Istiaia-Edipsos who are interested in alternative forms of tourism such as hiking and mountaineering. The routes run through mountainous, rural, and less tourist-developed areas away from the busy coastal zone, with the goal of strengthening the local economies of the small villages in these regions.
comments: equivalent works for Mantoudi Agia Anna were approved by the same formal approval and are actually referred in A9

titleRaw: Hydrological study for mountain and lowland works in North Evia
displayTitle: Hydrological study for mountain and lowland works in North Evia
description: The project achieves the following: it records all water supply and irrigation needs of the two municipalities of northern Euboea; assesses the volume of water that existing technical excavations from magnesite mining can collect so they may be used as permanent reservoirs; and identifies all sites proposed to date for the construction of water storage dams together with their storage capacities. It also supports the efficient organization of water resources and the preparation of a water management programme with a corresponding timetable, while integrating all previous actions in this field into a coherent and unified water policy.
comments: It is a study, not construction works are made with this

titleRaw: Prevention_Forest protection, Goldammer Committee report, Actions for the mobilisation of the local communities
displayTitle: Prevention_Forest protection, Goldammer Committee report, Actions for the mobilisation of the local communities
comments: The project covers the actions that must be taken before the fire season to reduce the likelihood of wildfires, make them easier to contain, limit potential damage in the event of a major fire, and improve the organization and preparedness of the authorities involved for the immediate response to such incidents. Funding is now being sought for the implementation of the programme.

titleRaw: Training – awareness raising – mobilisation of professionals and the local population
displayTitle: Training – awareness raising – mobilisation of professionals and the local population
description: This project was implemented with funding from other sources, both from the state and the Regional Government, and is excluded from the Reconstruction of Evia program.
comments:
The project supports 196 businesses in tourism, leisure and the creative industries, including 17 operating exclusively in the cultural and creative sectors.
Indicative training topics:
_Cultural resource management and use
_Event organisation
_Cultural marketing
_Circular economy, construction and crafts
_Culture and agri-food
_Two programmes will be delivered:
A) A 250-hour programme for 50 unemployed participants, including 150 hours of theory and 100 hours of practical training.
B) A 150-hour programme for 25 employees, self-employed professionals and entrepreneurs in the cultural and creative sectors.
